pr.title: More dups and a ft link
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Continuation of PR #2572 that added more decomp matches and data/linkage cleanup across fighter capture-Kirby code and many stage files. The most explicit linkage change was moving ftCo_CaptureKirby.c and ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, with splits/symbols adjusted so CaptureKirby, CaptureWaitKirby, and ThrownKirby own the correct .sdata/.sdata2 ranges and local literals. Stage changes mainly introduced or reshaped StageData-plus-string wrapper structs and reused embedded string offsets in OSReport calls to improve data matching. Bot metrics reported +4304 bytes matched code, +2116 bytes linked code, +72 bytes matched data, +88 bytes linked data, and 18 new matches.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureKirby.c and ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c are built as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Adjusted GALE01 splits so ftCo_CaptureKirby.c .sdata ends at 0x804D3BC8, while ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c owns .sdata 0x804D3BC8-0x804D3BE0 and ftCo_ThrownKirby.c owns .sdata 0x804D3BE0-0x804D3BF8.
Converted several config/GALE01/symbols.txt entries from global lbl_/ftCo_ address names to compiler-style local @NNN symbols, including CaptureKirby/Wait/ThrownKirby string and literal data.
Cleaned ftCo_CaptureKirby.c includes, removed placeholder/platform and unused headers, removed local literal definitions that became local symbols, reorganized inlines, and added PAD_STACK calls.
Moved ftCommon_InitGrab out of inlineA0 and called it directly in ftCo_800BD1DC after inlineA0, contributing to the function reaching 100% according to the bot report.
Refactored ftCo_800BD39C helper logic with stack padding and a padded local struct around Vec3 scale to improve matching.
Removed extern char* literal declarations from ftCo_ThrownKirby.c for ftCo_804D3BE0, ftCo_804D3BE8, and ftCo_804D3BF0 after the .sdata ownership/symbol-scope adjustment.

Tweaked ftKb_SpecialNYs.c stack layout in ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture2_0_Anim and ftKb_YsSpecialAirN2_1_Anim by replacing FORCE_PAD_STACK/PAD_STACK combinations with explicit u8 _pad[8] and larger PAD_STACK values.

Added or reshaped stage data/string layouts in many gr files, often introducing a local StageData wrapper struct with trailing report-format or filename strings, such as grBb_StageData, grGr_StageData, grHr_StageData, grIm_StageData, grKg_StageData, grMc_StageData, grOk_StageData, grOp_StageData, and grPu_StageData.

Changed multiple stage OSReport calls from direct string literals or __FILE__ usage to references into known data objects or explicit file-name strings, preserving original data layout and improving .data matching.

Reworked grVenom_80205AD4 and grVenom_80206874 control flow with explicit goto labels for spawn/link/default/done paths, and updated comments from low/partial match percentages to near-matching register-allocation TODOs.
Made small ordering/layout tweaks such as moving the Item* local declaration in it_8026EECC_inline_1 after Vec3* new_pos.
smart_moves: Promoting only the now-matching fighter translation units in configure.py made the linkage improvement explicit and measurable.
Splitting .sdata ownership between CaptureKirby, CaptureWaitKirby, and ThrownKirby fixed symbol placement instead of papering over mismatches with cross-file extern literals.
Using compiler-style local @NNN symbol names for file-local strings and literals reduced false global symbols and better reflected object-file-local data.
Wrapping StageData with trailing string arrays matched contiguous stage data blobs without inventing unrelated globals.
Rewriting OSReport calls to point at existing data/object offsets reused real embedded strings and helped stage .data matching.
Using small stack-layout nudges, including PAD_STACK and padded local structs, addressed last-byte function mismatches without broad behavioral rewrites.
For grVenom, explicit goto-labeled control flow preserved C readability enough to document paths while forcing code shape closer to the target assembly.
decomp_lessons: When a file owns adjacent literals that previously appeared as global address symbols, revisit splits.txt and symbols.txt together; correct section boundaries can be required before a source file links as Matching.
Do not keep extern declarations for literals that are actually local to another object or have been moved into the correct owning translation unit.
StageData objects in Melee stage files may be part of a larger contiguous data record that includes filename/report strings; model that as a wrapper struct when the data layout requires it.
OSReport literals can be sensitive to whether the compiler emits direct strings, __FILE__, or pointers into existing data; matching may require reusing embedded string offsets.
Near-100% stage/fighter functions may need non-semantic declaration ordering, temporary reuse, stack padding, or control-flow reshaping rather than algorithmic changes.
For register-allocation-sensitive functions, storing through the struct field and then reloading, as seen in grVenom other-gobj handling, can be a useful matching tactic when evidence supports it.
Small source ordering changes, including local variable declaration order, can affect item/fighter inline codegen.
naming_conventions: Compiler-local data symbols in symbols.txt use @NNN names with scope:local rather than lbl_ or module-prefixed global names when the data is file-local.
Stage wrapper structs followed the stage prefix plus StageData pattern, e.g. grBb_StageData, grGr_StageData, grHr_StageData, grIm_StageData, grKg_StageData.
Stage data globals retain address-style project names such as grBb_803E2D20 and grKg_803E1800 when semantic names are not known.
File-local arrays that should not export symbols may be declared static const, as in grCs_803B7F28 and grCs_803B7F3C.
Goto labels in grVenom used function-address-prefixed names with path suffixes, e.g. venom_80205AD4_spawn, venom_80205AD4_link, venom_80205AD4_default, venom_80205AD4_done.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use PAD_STACK or explicit u8 padding locals to reproduce stack frame size/alignment for stubborn fighter animation functions.
Use a padded local struct containing Vec3 when the target stack slot for a Vec3 appears offset from the natural local layout.
Reorder local declarations and initial assignments, such as initializing zero before other locals or moving Item* after Vec3*, to influence register allocation.
Replace structured nested if/else with explicit goto labels when the target branch layout requires a specific fallthrough and join shape.
Access struct fields through casts or raw stores when the known typed field layout does not yet generate the target code shape, as shown by grVenom clearing a flag via grCorneria_GroundVars2 cast.
Point OSReport arguments at existing data symbols or offsets rather than newly emitted string literals when matching data layout and relocations.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code improved to 70.01% (+0.11%, +4304 bytes) and linked code to 42.35% (+0.05%, +2116 bytes).

The bot reported 18 new matches, including ftCo_800BD39C, grKongo_801D5340, CaptureWaitKirby .sdata, ThrownKirby .sdata, grMuteCity_801EFD0C, grOldKongo_8020F52C, ftCo_800BD1DC, two ftKb_SpecialNYs animation functions, and several stage functions.
The bot reported notable unmatched-item improvements in stage .data sections, especially grkongo .data (+425 bytes, 41.39% to 93.47%) and groldkongo .data (+158 bytes, 31.25% to 91.24%).
The bot noted one unmatched-item regression for grbigblue grBigBlue_801E6364 with no byte change and unchanged rounded percent.
searchable_terms: More dups and a ft link
follow_up_queries: Look at PR #2572 to understand the prior duplicate-data work this PR continued.
Search for remaining stage files with StageData followed by adjacent report-format strings that could use wrapper structs.
Audit remaining ftCommon Kirby capture/throw files for incorrect .sdata/.sdata2 ownership or globalized local literals.
Search for OSReport calls using __FILE__ or direct literals in nearly-matching stage functions.
Investigate remaining grVenom register-allocation TODOs after the goto reshaping.
